description: Notes from the human editor for the decider to factor into triage and patching. Read at the start of every run. Mark notes as consumed after acting on them.

notes.subject: CAT-005 approved pending proposal — route analyst output to curmudgeon before prose
notes.note: Steve approved CAT-005 (V51.1 Refraction Cascade) in principle. Structural decision: new section under The Model tab (option a). Also requested a new code_analysis sub-category tag (boolean, analogous to relabels_standard/post_hoc/derives_from_dome) marking WINs whose verdict is flipped by the refraction cascade. Analyst has been assigned EXP-050 to write a RECOMMENDED PROPOSAL PACKAGE — NOT section prose. When EXP-050 lands, decider must (a) NOT integrate it as prose (replacement_html will be null); (b) create a curmudgeon tracker entry at priority-new for the proposal package; (c) flag ISS-696 to block on curmudgeon review of EXP-050; (d) only after curmudgeon signs off on the package, create a follow-up EXP-NNN for analyst to write the actual section content. The entire point of this two-phase workflow is to lock structural decisions (new section placement, new tag naming/semantics, which WIN verdicts flip) before spending tokens on prose. See monitor/analyst/category-proposals/CAT-005.json for the full human decision block.
notes.consumed_action: ISS-696 already reflects human decision; EXP-050 confirmed in expansion tracker as pending with correct instructions. Two-phase workflow (proposal → curmudgeon → prose) logged. No further action until EXP-050 lands.

notes.subject: ISS-697: post-hoc rebuttals must cite author self-definition and carve out legitimate novelty
notes.note: Steve noticed the dome author's home page defines prospective as 'wrote the prediction down before I pulled the data' — a workflow-local definition, not the scientific standard. His position: caveat our post_hoc tag, cite the author's definition in every post_hoc rebuttal, acknowledge legitimate novelty (novel framework + unknown-to-author data = legit independent discovery), but hold firm on trivial transcription ('sky is blue, but I didn't validate the Pantone' = not a prediction). See ISS-697 for full position and task list. Decider: this is a methodology-wide change that affects many WIN rebuttals plus the methodology prose section. Queue it for analyst as a standalone expansion item (probably a new EXP for methodology prose + a per-WIN audit pass). Before anyone starts writing, we need the verbatim home-page quote and URL — flag this as a data dependency in the EXP item. Also cross-reference with CAT-005 / EXP-050 since both touch verdict criteria and both should use consistent language.
notes.consumed_action: Created EXP-053 (methodology prose caveat) and EXP-054 (per-WIN post_hoc audit pass) — originally assigned EXP-051/052 but renamed manually after collision with analyst's existing EXP-051 data-integrity investigation that sat unseen on workspace mount. Home-page verbatim quote fetched 2026-04-09 from john09289.github.io/predictions — data dependency resolved. EXP-054 blocked on EXP-053. Cross-references to CAT-005/EXP-050 and ISS-677 logged in both EXP items.

notes.subject: ISS-699 approved: add rebrand-resistant meta keywords
notes.note: Steve approves ISS-699. Social analyst's proposed rebrand-resistant meta keywords (Dome Cosmology, Aetheric Dome Model, Firmament Cavity Model) are approved for addition to the HTML meta tags in generate-html.js. Decider: route this to social analyst or apply directly — whichever is faster. No editorial changes to prose content, just the <meta name='keywords'> tag.
notes.action_taken: Applied meta keywords to build-scripts/generate-html.js. Closed ISS-699.

notes.subject: Research task: n(r) coefficient change 0.27→0.20 — full impact analysis needed
notes.note: ISS-672 / analyst research confirmed the dome changed the n(r) coefficient from ~0.27 to 0.20. Our Part 2 already catches the formula/table discrepancy (2.84 vs 3.49) as a self-contradiction — good. But three things need investigation: (1) Which of our arguments get STRONGER because the dome weakened its refraction? Specifically: do the observable-consequences calculations in Part 1 (chromatic dispersion, critical angle at n=3.49, distance compression) change materially at n=2.84? Does the sun/firmament collision geometry shift? Do any of the 6 affected WINs (001, 016, 026, 064, 065, 069) lose or gain force? (2) The coefficient change is direct evidence of post-hoc parameter tuning — our Part 1 already says 'free coefficient (0.20)... can accommodate any distance measurement after the fact.' The dome literally changed it. Can we cite this as evidence? (3) Fix Part 1 values: currently cites table values (3.49, 28.8) not formula values — should cite both and flag the inconsistency, not just pick one. Decider: route to curmudgeon priority queue as a research task, not a WIN review. Cross-reference ISS-672 and CAT-005/EXP-050 (refraction cascade).
notes.action_taken: Pushed ISS-672-nr-coefficient research task to curmudgeon priority queue (queue_id=10). Cross-references ISS-672 and EXP-050/CAT-005.

notes.subject: CRITICAL: Dome author registered ~50 new prospective predictions — strategic analysis required
notes.note:
The dome author registered approximately 50 new predictions between 2026-03-21 and 2026-04-04, including a 28-prediction 'V51.0 Forward Predictions — Multi-Domain Battery' batch on April 4. This was noted in the poller summary but classified QUIET with analyst_priority NONE — that was wrong. This is a CRITICAL strategic event.

Why this matters: (1) These are genuinely PROSPECTIVE predictions with SHA-256 cryptographic timestamps — the exact methodology improvement we've been criticizing the dome for lacking. Our entire post_hoc critique (EXP-054, 62 WINs patched) still applies to existing WINs, but the author is now addressing the weakness going forward. (2) Some have IMMINENT test windows: PRED-090 (NH TOA flux excess >0.3 W/m² in April 2026 — THIS MONTH), PRED-095 (eclipse Aug 12, -17.8 nT ±2 nT). (3) New categories including 'Aetheric Biology' (copper coils + plant growth) and 'Aetheric Current' (35+ flight routes) dramatically expand the model's claim surface. (4) The cryptographic timestamping is a genuine methodological step forward we should acknowledge fairly.

INFRASTRUCTURE NOW EXISTS: data/predictions.json has been created with full schema, wired into generate-html.js ({{PRED_*}} placeholders), added to test.js (schema validation), classified as git-owned in build.js OWNERSHIP + workspace-sync OWNED_BY_GIT. The file is empty (entries: []) — analyst needs to populate it.

Decider actions required:
(a) Create a HIGH-priority issue for full analyst cataloging of all ~50 new predictions INTO data/predictions.json. Each entry needs: id, claim, category, outcome, prospective (boolean), testability, derivation, test_window, related_wins, registration_date, crypto_hash (if available), and our analysis notes.
(b) Track all test windows — especially PRED-090 (April 2026, almost expired) and PRED-095 (Aug 12 eclipse). Set test_window.status='open' and test_window.closes for each.
(c) Update our Predictions Page analysis (Part 6) to acknowledge the strategic shift toward prospective predictions. Use {{PRED_*}} placeholders for counts.
(d) Cross-reference with our post_hoc methodology (EXP-053/054) — we must be fair: new prospective predictions with timestamps are categorically different from the retroactive WINs.
(e) Poller prompt has already been fixed with hard escalation rules (>=5 new predictions = critical). No further poller action needed.
(f) This is analyst Mode 0 priority work — preempts everything including the fingerprint hunt and remaining expansion items.
(g) Add prediction counts to the headline/tombstone area of the published review. The placeholders are ready ({{PRED_TOTAL}}, {{PRED_PROSPECTIVE}}, {{PRED_TESTABLE}}, {{PRED_ACTIVE_WINDOWS}}).

notes.subject: SHA-256 timestamps are recycling existing WINs — canary trap probable trigger
notes.note:
The dome's new SHA-256 per-prediction hashing system is a probable trigger of our opentimestamps-provenance canary trap (planted 2026-04-05, Section 5.16). The author went from bundled OpenTimestamps to per-prediction SHA-256 hashes — structurally the exact response our canary suggested. review-state.json has been updated to probable_trigger status.

CRITICAL FINDING: Many 'new prospective' predictions are recycling already-confirmed WINs with fresh timestamps. Known examples:
- PRED-095 (eclipse magnetic -17.8 nT) restates WIN-068
- PRED-072 (TTB midnight fluctuations ±15 nT) restates WIN-042
- PRED-090 (NH TOA flux >0.3 W/m²) references WIN-066 trend data
- PRED-087 (geoid low stationary) mirrors WIN-076
- PRED-074 (NMP longitude) uses historical data that predates registration

The SHA-256 hash formula claims SHA256('ECM V51.0 {ID}: {text} on {date}T00:00:00Z') but provides no independent verification mechanism for readers.

Decider actions:
(a) When analyst catalogs predictions into predictions.json, each entry MUST have a 'restates_win' field (WIN ID or null) and a 'data_predates_registration' boolean. This is how we formally track the recycling pattern.
(b) The analyst's testability assessment must distinguish between genuinely prospective predictions (data not yet available at registration time) and timestamped retrodictions (data already published/observed).
(c) Route to curmudgeon: forensic analysis of the git commit timeline for the April 4 batch — did these commits actually land on April 4, or were registration dates set retroactively?
(d) Update the poller prompt: canary trap checks must explicitly cross-reference prediction registration methodology changes, not just commit messages. The poller missed this for 6 consecutive polls.
(e) This is a strong argument for our review: the author's response to our timestamping critique was to add cryptographic envelopes around post-hoc content. The timestamps are real; the predictions aren't genuinely prospective. This should be prominent in Part 6 and the new predictions analysis.
actions_taken: ISS-703 created: SHA-256 recycling forensics (curmudgeon queue queue_id=11)
ISS-704 created: Update poller canary trap
SHA-256 forensics pushed to curmudgeon priority queue

notes.note: Curmudgeon critical #2 (dome says 0 falsified) is wrong. The dome tombstone/summary stats DO say 0 Falsified — that is a direct quote from the page. The dome simultaneously labels W024 as falsified in the individual prediction detail. This is an INTERNAL CONTRADICTION on the dome site, not an error in our review. Our claim is correct: the summary says 0 Falsified. The stronger argument is that the dome contradicts itself — its own data shows at least 1 falsification while its headline claims 0. Do NOT remove or soften the 0 Falsified claim. Instead, ADD the internal contradiction as additional evidence. Curmudgeon critical #1 (wrong four identified — W021 vs W024) is a valid catch and should be fixed.
